title: sarothamnus vulgaris var. scoparius
intro: Sarothamnus vulgaris var. scoparius, also known as broom or common broom, is a shrub native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of broom include relief from digestive issues, such as diarrhea and dyspepsia, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and improve respiratory problems. Broom can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of health conditions.
